patterns.name: npm Access Token
patterns.risk: Can publish packages to npm under your account. Supply chain attack vector.
patterns.fix: Use NPM_TOKEN environment variable. Set up npm 2FA.

patterns.name: PyPI API Token
patterns.risk: Can upload packages to PyPI. Critical supply chain attack vector.
patterns.fix: Use TWINE_PASSWORD environment variable with trusted publishing.

patterns.name: Docker Hub Access Token
patterns.risk: Can push/pull images, manage repositories on Docker Hub.

patterns.name: Vercel Token
patterns.risk: Can deploy, manage, and access all Vercel projects.

patterns.name: Netlify Access Token
patterns.risk: Can deploy sites, manage DNS, and access build logs.

patterns.name: Heroku API Key
patterns.risk: Full access to Heroku apps — deploy, scale, access databases, read config vars.

patterns.name: Terraform Cloud API Token
patterns.risk: Can manage infrastructure state, trigger applies, access sensitive variables.

patterns.name: CircleCI API Token
patterns.description: CircleCI personal or project API token
patterns.risk: Can trigger builds, access build artifacts, and read environment variables.

patterns.name: Sentry Auth Token
patterns.risk: Can create/modify Sentry projects, access issue data, and manage org settings depending on scopes.
patterns.fix: Use SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN environment variable. Rotate immediately if exposed.
false_positive_hints: Ignore clearly truncated or placeholder values in docs

patterns.name: Sentry DSN
patterns.description: Sentry Data Source Name (DSN) used by SDKs to submit events
patterns.risk: Attackers can inject fake error events into your Sentry project (noise/DoS of quotas).
false_positive_hints: Ignore localhost or example DSN placeholders in docs
